architecture.summary: A neural network model trained on psychophysical tasks receives natural language instructions embedded by a pretrained language model. The model learns to map linguistic instructions to task-relevant sensorimotor representations, achieving zero-shot generalization to unseen tasks. Language scaffolds the activity such that interrelated tasks share common geometric structure with instruction embeddings, allowing composition of practiced skills. The model can also generate linguistic descriptions of novel tasks from motor feedback alone, which can guide partner models.

biblio.title: Natural language instructions induce compositional generalization in networks of neurons

claims.statement: Models can perform a previously unseen task with an average performance of 83% correct based solely on linguistic instructions (zero-shot learning)
Language scaffolds sensorimotor representations such that activity for interrelated tasks shares a common geometry with the semantic representations of instructions
Models can generate a linguistic description of a novel task identified using only motor feedback, which can subsequently guide a partner model to perform the task
The model offers experimentally testable predictions outlining how linguistic information must be represented to facilitate flexible and general cognition in the human brain
